data.title: Toro tumbles as Northland downgrades after full-year forecast cut
data.description:
Toro (NYSE:TTC) -6.1% to its lowest in a month in Friday's trading and down more than 9% in two days after reporting slightly better than expected Q2 adjusted earnings and slightly lower than estimated revenues, but the lawnmower maker trimmed its full-year earnings and sales projections due to "anticipated tariff impacts."

For FY 2025, Toro (NYSE:TTC) now sees earnings of $4.15-$4.30, below the $4.31 analyst consensus estimate and down from prior guidance of $4.25-$4.40, and forecasts full-year revenues coming in flat to up 3% from FY 2024 to $4.58B-$4.72B, in line with $4.62B consensus.

Toro (NYSE:TTC) attributed the downbeat view in part to "anticipated tariff impacts," including "a reduction in volume from macro factors that have driven increased homeowner and channel caution."

Northland Securities downgraded shares to Market Perform from Outperform with an $80 price target, cut from $100, citing the mixed Q2 results and lowered guidance driven by tariffs and weakening residential demand.

Despite the challenges, Northland analyst Ted Jackson sees minimal risk of further decline in Toro's (TTC) share value from its current standing.

data.title: SA Asks: What are the best gaming stocks right now?
data.description:
What are the best gaming stocks right now for investors?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Kenio Fontes and Daniel Jones for their picks.

Kenio Fontes: Two companies stand out for me in the industry, Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOF) and Take-Two Interactive (NASDAQ:TTWO), and both have interesting catalysts. 

Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOF) has an undeniable moat when compared to its peers. The competition for it is very different because it dominates a niche and differentiates itself with both its hardware and exclusive games, managing to exploit its IPs and loyal fan base very well. The catalyst is the Nintendo Switch 2, which should maintain the company's momentum, as pre-sales show.

Take-Two (NASDAQ:TTWO), on the other hand, has the launch of Grand Theft Auto VI in 2026, which is arguably the most anticipated game in history. In terms of financials, it should bring in a few billion in revenue in the first year and expand the margin.

Although growth prospects and moats make them appealing alternatives for the long term, the problem for these two is that much is already priced into the valuation.

Daniel Jones: While known far more for its cloud computing and work-related software applications, Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) is a major player in the global video game industry. Between 2024 and 2030, the video game space is expected to grow from $217.1 billion to $583.7 billion. As the owner of the Xbox platform, Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stands to benefit from this expansion. 

According to some estimates, by February of last year, the company’s Game Pass service had grown to 34 million users. This year it's expected to hit 50 million. With the service starting at only $9.99 per month and opening the door to gamers through the provision of over 400 first and third-party games, it represents an attractive value proposition for gamers and for Microsoft (MSFT) shareholders.

Unfortunately, since gaming represents only a small portion of the company's overall revenue, it's not considered a pure play video game prospect. In fact, most of the value moving forward will come from Microsoft's core operations, like what it's doing on the cloud computing front. This could limit the appeal for investors who want more of a pure play player in the video game industry. But for those who want diversification and attractive growth at a good price, Microsoft (MSFT) is certainly worth considering. 

One of the more interesting prospects in the video game industry these days is Take-Two Interactive (TTWO). The company has both good and bad aspects to it. 

On the one hand, from 2023 through 2025, revenue expanded from $5.34 billion to $5.63 billion. But the firm also generated an aggregate net loss during this window of time of $9.35 billion.  Admittedly, $5.89 billion of this came from impairment charges, while the company spent another $211.1 million on business reorganization costs. Clearly, this is a company that has some issues.

On the other hand, there is opportunity here. While the company has a number of leading franchises such as Red Dead Redemption, its upcoming installment of the Grand Theft Auto franchise has been highly anticipated. The most successful of these was Grand Theft Auto V, which was released in 2013 and has since sold over 210 million units across the globe. Analysts estimate that, in the first year, the newest installment, Grand Theft Auto VI, will sell 40 million units and generate $3.2 billion in revenue.

However, it has become a running joke that certain things that are improbable or far off into the future will occur before that game comes out. The latest news from management was that instead of coming out in the fall of this year, the game will now be released in May 2026. But seeing as how planning for the game did not start until 2018 and principal production did not begin until 2020, it wouldn't be surprising to see such a long-lasting project take longer to get completed.

Naturally, this could represent significant upside potential for shareholders of the business. But when stacked against the bottom-line results of the company in recent years, it certainly makes it a riskier opportunity. 

  • Top Interactive Home Entertainment Stocks

data.title: Federal Reserve taps Michael Horowitz as new inspector general
data.description:
The Federal Reserve appointed Michael E. Horowitz to lead the U.S. central bank's Office of Inspector General starting June 30, 2025, it said on Friday.

The new OIG will also serve in that same role for the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, which is financed by the Fed.

With 35 years' experience in law, public administration and proves, Horowitz will be tasked with making recommendations to improve agency efficiency and effectiveness, as well as identifying and preventing waste, fraud and abuse, the Fed said in a release.

He is succeeding Mark Bialek, who retired in April, after nearly 14 years as inspector general.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Apple's WWDC event, Chime IPO, BlackRock's Investor Day and fresh inflation reads
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - June 9

  • Volatility watch - Applied Digital (APLD) and Ondas Holdings (ONDS) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include SafeSpace Global (OTCPK:SSGC), M3-Brigade Acquisition V. (MBAV), and Regulus Therapeutics (RGLS). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Brown-Forman Corporation (BF.A), Abacus Global Management (ABL) and Arrive AI (ARAI).
  • IPO watch - San Francisco-based fintech company Chime Financial (CHYM) is expected to price its IPO and begin trading. At the top end of the expected range, Chime could raise up to $832 million, with a fully diluted valuation of about $11 billion. Voyager Technologies (VOYG) is also set to launch its IPO. The Denver-based aerospace and defense company is targeting a valuation of approximately $1.6 billion. The quiet period ends on eToro Group (ETOR) to free up analysts to post ratings. The IPO lock-up period expires for certain blocks of shares of ServiceTitan (TTAN).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Casey's General Stores (CASY), Calavo Growers (CVGW), and Lakeland Industries (LAKE).
  • Dividend watch - Some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL), Travelers (TRV), Kohl's (KSS), and Pimco (PCN).
  • All day - Apple (AAPL) will hold its WWDC event in Cupertino, California. The event is expected to feature major announcements and a first look at new updates for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS, tvOS, and visionOS.
  • All day - Cisco (CSCO) holds its flagship annual conference over a four-day period. Keynote presentations include talks from top executives, including CEO Chuck Robbins and other senior leaders, focusing on AI, cybersecurity, networking, and cloud solutions.
  • All day - Databricks Data + AI Summit will feature talks from top executives from Databricks, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, and Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella.

 

Tuesday - June 10

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include GameStop (GME), J.M. Smucker (SJM), United Natural Foods (UNFI), and Gitlab (GTLB). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Stitch Fix (SFIX) and Dave & Buster's (PLAY) after their reports drop.
  • All day - Nvidia (NVDA) will participate in the Nasdaq Investor Conference in partnership with Jefferies.
  • All day - O'Reilly Auto (ORLY) will begin trading on a post-split basis following the 15-for-1 stock split.
  • All day - Datadog (DDOG) will hold its flagship two-day Dash 2025 event.
  • 9:20 a.m. Amgen's (AMGN) CFO and R&D chief will discuss company strategy and recent developments at the Goldman Sachs Global Healthcare Conference.
  • 10:00 a.m. Merck (MRK) will participate in a fireside chat at the Goldman Sachs Global Healthcare Conference.

 

Wednesday - June 11

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Oracle (ORCL), Chewy (CHWY), and Sailpoint (SAIL). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Vera Bradley (VRA) after its report is released.
  • All day - Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Dana (DAN), Aptiv (APTV), Mobileye (MBLY), SES AI (SES), and VinFast Auto (VFS) are some of the notable companies presenting at the Deutsche Bank Global Auto Industry Conference.
  • All day - Target (TGT) will hold its annual shareholder meeting.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Consumer Price Index report for May will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists will be digging in to see if there was an impact of tariff policy on prices.
  • 9:15 a.m. AT&T (T) will webcast a fireside chat with CFO Pascal Desroches at the 2025 Mizuho Technology Conference.
  • 10:00 a.m. Nvidia (NVDA) will participate in Rosenblatt's 5th Annual Technology Summit: The Age of AI.

 

Thursday - June 12

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Adobe (ADBE) and Lovesac (LOVE).
  • All day - The four-day European Hematology Association (EHA) Congress in Milan, Italy, will begin. Nurix Therapeutics (NRIX), Incyte (INCY) and Gilead Sciences (GILD) are some of the healthcare stocks that have been singled out by analysts to watch.
  • All day - ADP (ADP) will host an Investor Day. During the event, members of the ADP senior management team will discuss the company's strategic priorities and financial outlook.
  • All day - BlackRock (BLK) will hold its Investor Day event.
  • All day - Cardinal Health (CAH) will hold an Investor Day event.
  • All day - Tesla (TSLA) is expected to launch its robotaxi service in Austin, Texas. The rollout will start with a small fleet, initially about 10 Model Y vehicles, operating in a limited, geofenced area of Austin.
  • 8:30 a.m. The producer price index report for May will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.
  • 12:30 p.m. AMD (AMD) CEO Dr. Lisa Su will give a keynote talk at the company's Advancing AI event. Other speakers include representatives from Amazon (AMZN), Netflix (NFLX), and Dell Technologies (DELL).
  • 12:30 p.m. Arista Networks (ANET) will host an event titled "Demystifying AI Networking."

 

Friday - June 13

  • 10:00 a.m. The release of the preliminary University of Michigan survey for June will be closely watched by economists for its update on consumer sentiment and inflation expectations.

data.title: From peak bromance to pure chaos: Timeline of Trump and Elon's relationship
data.description:
From hurling insults at each other on social media to dropping the Jeffrey Epstein “bomb,” the escalating war of words between U.S. President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk is a stunning example of a celebrated bromance that seemingly turned into a brutal clash overnight.

The dramatic brawl started when Musk, who once joined Trump on his presidential campaign and spent at least $250 million to help elect him and other Republican candidates, blasted the president’s "big beautiful bill" of tax breaks and spending cuts as a "disgusting abomination."

As a result of this shocking number of insults traded back and forth, even investors started selling Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares, concerned about new headwinds for the electric vehicle maker. Musk’s Tesla fell over 14% on Thursday, marking one of its worst declines in history.

Here is a rundown of the Trump-Musk era:

July 2024

Musk endorses Trump as the next president after the latter survived an assassination attempt in Butler, Pa.

"I fully endorse President Trump and hope for his rapid recovery," wrote Musk. 

Musk said he donated to Trump’s campaign and that, “Last time America had a candidate this tough was Theodore Roosevelt.”

August 2024

Musk scheduled a friendly interview with Trump on X. However, the interview was marred by technical glitches, delaying its start. Both of them exchanged praise repeatedly, signalling a cozier relationship.

Musk even said that he is “willing to serve” in government, as per an X post, showing his own image as a representative of DOGE, an acronym for the Department of Government Efficiency.

October 2024

Musk appeared at a Pennsylvania rally to endorse Trump, the same place where Trump survived the assassination attempt. Wearing a cap with the “Make America Great Again”, the tech billionaire said “this will be the last election” if Trump doesn’t win. 

Musk also promised to give $1M daily until Election Day to voters registered in swing states who sign an online petition supporting the First and Second Amendments, leading to a DOJ warning.

November 2024

Trump gets elected to a second term.

"The people of America gave Trump a crystal clear mandate for change. The future is gonna be fantastic," Musk posted on X.  Musk's net worth has crossed $300 billion for the first time in nearly three years, while Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) crossed over the $1T market cap level in November.

Trump praised Musk and said, “We have a new star, a star is born: Elon.” 

On November 12, Trump named Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy to head up a new Department of Government Efficiency.

February–March 2025

Musk and Trump appeared together in the Oval Office, along with Musk's son, and jointly answered questions from the press.

The Trump administration and DOGE spearheaded the effort to eliminate USAID, slashed staff and took aim at the consumer protection agency, with Trump calling Musk "tremendously successful".

Trump also defended Musk’s company when it faced major backlash, saying he would buy a "brand new Tesla."

April 2025

Reports started floating that Musk will step back from lead government role.

Musk reportedly also made personal appeals to Trump to reverse the sweeping tariffs.

May 2025

Musk steps down from DOGE after he criticized Trump's "big, beautiful" budget bill. In an interview with CBS, Musk said the “big, beautiful bill” will not help the nation’s finances.

“I was, like, disappointed to see the massive spending bill, frankly, which increases the budget deficit, not just decrease it, and undermines the work that the DOGE team is doing,” Musk said in a clip.

Trump gave a farewell to Musk in the Oval Office on May 30.

June 5

Musk lashes out at Trump’s bill.

"This massive, outrageous, pork-filled Congressional spending bill is a disgusting abomination. Shame on those who voted for it: you know you did wrong. You know it," Musk posted on X.

The tension quickly escalated during Trump’s meeting with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, where Trump expressed his disappointment in Musk, saying, "Elon and I had a great relationship, I don’t know if we will anymore."

After a shocking number of back-and-forth insults on both X and Trump’s Social Truth platform, Musk posted on X saying, “Time to drop the really big bomb: Trump is in the Epstein files. That is the real reason they have not been made public. Have a nice day, DJT!”

Trump told ABC News on Friday morning that he wasn't interested in speaking with Musk, adding that Musk had "lost his mind" and threatened to pull Musk's contracts and subsidies.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: MasTec raised on strong pipeline opportunities, MYR Group lowered at Goldman
data.description:
MasTec (NYSE:MTZ) +2.5% in Friday's trading as Goldman Sachs upgrades shares to Buy from Neutral with a $195 price target, raised from $156, citing significant new pipeline construction opportunities from midstream oil and gas companies, positioning the company for significant growth.

MasTec is poised for a "strong backlog increase" as one of the leading pipeline contractors in the U.S., with seven new pipelines recently receiving final investment decisions, Goldman's Ati Modak wrote, estimating $2.4B-$2.5B in annual revenues for the pipeline segment through 2030, an increase from the bank's previous assumption of ~$2B.

Given that MasTec's (NYSE:MTZ) highest EBITDA margin segment is Pipeline Infrastructure, Modak revised its EBITDA estimates to up 2%, up 8%, and up 3% above FactSet consensus for 2025, 2026 and 2027, respectively.

Also, new framework agreements are expected to drive improved operational visibility and margins in MasTec's (MTZ) Clean Energy segment, the analyst said.

MYR Group (MYRG) is a "pure play means of gaining SMID exposure to transmission and distribution," Modak wrote, but the analyst downgraded shares to Neutral from Buy with a $168 PT, believing the stock likely is pricing in expected growth at current levels.

data.title: Stocks rally on strong May jobs data, S&P 500 touches 6,000 for first time since Feb
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Friday jumped as sentiment was boosted by a strong May jobs report.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) topped the 6,000 points level for the first time since February 26, and was last +1.2% in afternoon trade. It last closed above that level on February 21.

Meanwhile, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last +1.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was +1%. 

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields were substantially higher. The 10-year yield (US10Y) rose 11 basis points to 4.50%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 12 basis points to 4.05%.

Before the opening bell, May Nonfarm payrolls showed an increase of +139K vs. +126K consensus and +147K prior (revised from +177K), according to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.

Also, the unemployment rate came in unchanged at 4.2%, in line with consensus.

“Friday's jobs report likely doesn't change the Federal Reserve's expected pause on interest rates at their next meeting later this month," said Glen Smith, CIO at GDS Wealth Management.

"Friday's jobs report shows slowing but still solid growth, which may warrant a rate cut at some point towards the end of the year, at the Fed's November or December meetings. Right now, there is little reason for the Fed to cut rates in the very immediate term thanks to solid economic data,” he said. 

“With heightened recession fears, we expect the employment and inflation data to take center stage for the near-term as investors try to assess any potential economic fallout from the current state of trade uncertainty,” Smith added.

Wall Street closed in the red on Thursday as Tesla (TSLA) shaved off more than $100B in market capitalization amid a public verbal spat between U.S. President Donald Trump and CEO Elon Musk.

There were some indications of a potential thaw in tensions between Musk and Trump on Friday.

Turning to trade developments, Reuters reported that China had granted temporary export licenses for rare earth suppliers to the top three U.S. automakers, citing two sources familiar with the matter. China's strict export controls over rare earths has been a flashpoint in U.S. trade relations.  

Trump on Thursday said he had held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the two nations.

“It's becoming clear that the rhetoric on tariffs is much tougher than the action. That is helping to improve market sentiment, as investors are getting more used to hard rhetoric on tariffs and are more accustomed to taking a wait and see approach,” GDS Wealth Management's Smith said.

data.title: Trump expected to extend TikTok ban....again
data.description:
• President Trump is expected to extend the deadline for a sale of TikTok, according to sources cited by The Wall Street Journal, as the June 19 deadline looms.
  • This would be the third time the president has postponed the enforcement of a congressionally mandated ban on TikTok, originally set to take effect in January. The president seeks to find a buyer – or buyers – for the platform, creating a new U.S.-based entity that would steer TikTok away from Chinese prevue.
  • Several investors, including Oracle (ORCL), Blackstone (BX), and Dell (DELL) have stepped forward to take a majority stake in TikTok, leaving ByteDance (BDNCE) with a diminished 20% stake.
  • However, with the U.S. in trade talks with China, a deal is unlikely to be hammered out before the mid-June deadline with ByteDance (BDNCE) conceding that any deal would be subject to approval by Chinse law.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: PLTR, INTC, C and SMCI
data.description:
Analysts at Seeking Alpha have made notable calls, including upgrades for Intel (INTC) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR), given their strategic initiatives and market momentum. Conversely, Super Micro Computer (SMCI) has been downgraded due to growth headwinds, and Citigroup’s (C) outlook has been adjusted due to macroeconomic risks.

Upgrades

  • Intel (NASDAQ:INTC): Upgrade to Buy by Hawkinvest. The analyst cites new CEO Lip-Bu Tan’s leadership as a catalyst for turnaround, with strategic positioning in onshoring, AI, quantum computing, and humanoid robotics creating long-term growth opportunities.

    “I think it makes sense to bet on [CEO Lip-Bu]Tan, and this is based on his past success as well as because of the strategic plans he has laid out for Intel. I saw management credibility issues at Intel before Mr. Tan’s arrival… Mr. Tan has already made changes that are designed to make the company lean and more focused, and I believe that in time, these changes will show up in the financial results.”

  • Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:PLTR): Upgrade to Buy by Deep Value Investing. Despite previously giving the stock a Hold rating, the analyst now sees Palantir as well-positioned due to its strong momentum in the US commercial segment and increased FY 2025 revenue guidance.

    “The growth potential from the adoption of the AIP platform by large enterprises outweighs the limited number of government contracts in the US or abroad… Overall, despite absurdly high valuation ratios (EV/EBITDA of 704, P/CF of 233), I rate Palantir as a buy.”

Downgrades

  • Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI): Downgrade to Hold by Michael Del Monte. The analyst identifies potential growth headwinds as the rack-scale design market becomes more commoditized with semiconductor designers bringing solutions in-house, coupled with margin pressures from shorter GPU cadences.

    “Given the potential growth headwinds SMCI may face, I believe an appropriate price target is $38.30/share at 0.85x eFY26 price/sales. With growth forecasts remaining relatively muted when compared to management’s $40b revenue target, I believe SMCI needs to prove the durability of its growth trajectory before shares reflect a higher growth trajectory.”

  • Citigroup Inc. (NYSE:C): Downgrade to Hold by Envision Research. The decision follows Berkshire Hathaway’s continued divestitures of banking stocks and concerns about worsening delinquency rates for various consumer loans amid ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties.

    “With the ongoing geopolitical and macroeconomic risks, I see large odds for the delinquency rates to persist at elevated levels or even further worsen. Under these conditions, I see BAC as a better choice given its better ROE and lower leverage compared to C. I expect these factors to make BAC more resilient, no matter what comes next.”

data.title: This biotech has posted 145-fold YTD jump with entire rally attributed to narrative
data.description:
Regencell Bioscience (RGCL), a Hong Kong-based biotech focused on traditional Chinese medicine, has surged in value this year, marking a 145-fold YTD rise, which one Seeking Alpha analyst characterized as "entirely narrative-driven."

SA analyst Grassroots Trading issued a Strong Sell rating on the stock last week, arguing that despite Regencell’s (NASDAQ:RGC) $8B market cap, the company has no revenue, clinical advancements, or strategic moves to justify its high valuation.  

RGC, focused on neurodevelopmental conditions such as autism spectrum disorder and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, “operates on anecdotal evidence and alternative medicine, lacking peer-reviewed data, clinical trials, or regulatory filings for its TCM-based ADHD/ASD treatments,” Grassroots Trading wrote.

Regencell’s tiny public float and insider lockups have led to an extremely volatile stock and speculative trading, the analyst opined.  

Only about 802.14K RGC shares, implying roughly 6% of 13M of its shares outstanding, are available in the public float, while the remainder are closely held by company insiders, who extended their lock-up agreements through April 2026 early this year, Grassroots Trading pointed out.

“To me, this is a narrative-driven symbol of speculation, where valuation is no longer anchored to fundamentals but inflated by hype, share scarcity, and ultimately, total delusion,” the analyst added.
